questions.title: Maximum Sum Subarray of Size K
questions.prompt:
Given an array of integers and a number k, find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray of size k.

You should avoid recalculating every subarray sum from scratch.

old_sum - leftmost + next</text></svg>
hints: First compute the sum of the first k elements.
Then slide the window one step at a time.
Add the new element entering the window.
Subtract the old element leaving the window.

def max_sum_subarray(arr, k):

    return 0

print(max_sum_subarray([2,1,5,1,3,2], 3))
print(max_sum_subarray([2,3,4,1,5], 2))
questions.solution_code:
def max_sum_subarray(arr, k):
    if k <= 0 or k > len(arr):
        return 0

    window_sum = sum(arr[:k])
    max_sum = window_sum

    for i in range(k, len(arr)):
        window_sum += arr[i]
        window_sum -= arr[i - k]
        max_sum = max(max_sum, window_sum)

    return max_sum

print(max_sum_subarray([2,1,5,1,3,2], 3))
print(max_sum_subarray([2,3,4,1,5], 2))

questions.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
questions.prompt:
Given a string, return the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

Use a moving window and shrink it whenever a duplicate appears.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Longest substring without repeating characters"><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.72)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="42" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters</text><text x="30" y="74" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Expand right. If duplicate appears, move left until window becomes valid again.</text><g font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="52" y="124" width="76" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.30)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="90" y="164" text-anchor="middle">a</text><rect x="140" y="124" width="76" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.30)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="178" y="164" text-anchor="middle">b</text><rect x="228" y="124" width="76" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.30)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="266" y="164" text-anchor="middle">c</text><rect x="316" y="124" width="76" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="354" y="164" text-anchor="middle">a</text><rect x="404" y="124" width="76" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="442" y="164" text-anchor="middle">b</text><rect x="492" y="124" width="76" height="62" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.96)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="530" y="164" text-anchor="middle">c</text></g><path d="M 52 214 L 304 214" stroke="#1f2328" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round"/><text x="178" y="246" text-anchor="middle" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Current valid window = "abc"</text><rect x="640" y="104" width="288" height="116" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.14)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="664" y="136" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Window set</text><text x="664" y="170" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">{ a, b, c }</text><text x="664" y="202" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">duplicate 'a' means move left</text><text x="30" y="286" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Best answer length = 3</text></svg>
hints: Use a set to track characters in the current window.
Move the right pointer forward.
If duplicate found, move left pointer until duplicate is removed.
Track the maximum window length.
questions.solution_code:
def longest_unique_substring(s):
    char_set = set()
    left = 0
    max_len = 0

    for right in range(len(s)):
        while s[right] in char_set:
            char_set.remove(s[left])
            left += 1

        char_set.add(s[right])
        max_len = max(max_len, right - left + 1)

    return max_len

print(longest_unique_substring("abcabcbb"))
print(longest_unique_substring("bbbbb"))

questions.title: Count Subarrays with Sum K
questions.prompt:
Given an array of integers and a value k, return the number of subarrays whose sum equals k.

This is often solved using prefix sums plus a hash map.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 980 320" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Count subarrays with sum K using prefix sum"><rect width="980" height="320" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.72)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="42" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Count Subarrays with Sum K</text><text x="30" y="74" font-size="16" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Count how many times prefix_sum - k has appeared before</text><g font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328"><rect x="52" y="122" width="80" height="60" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.24)"/><text x="92" y="161" text-anchor="middle">1</text><rect x="146" y="122" width="80" height="60" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.24)"/><text x="186" y="161" text-anchor="middle">1</text><rect x="240" y="122" width="80" height="60" rx="14" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.24)"/><text x="280" y="161" text-anchor="middle">1</text></g><text x="52" y="224" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">prefix sums: 1, 2, 3</text><rect x="530" y="104" width="390" height="132" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.14)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="554" y="138" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Prefix count map</text><text x="554" y="172" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">{ 0:1, 1:1, 2:1 }</text><text x="554" y="206" font-size="18" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">When prefix_sum = 2, prefix_sum - k = 0 found</text><text x="52" y="282" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Answer = 2</text></svg>
hints: Maintain a running prefix sum.
If prefix_sum - k has appeared before, add its frequency to the answer.
Use a dictionary to store prefix sum counts.
Initialize dictionary with {0: 1}.
questions.starter_code:
def count_subarrays_sum_k(nums, k):
    # TODO
    return 0

print(count_subarrays_sum_k([1,1,1], 2))
print(count_subarrays_sum_k([1,2,3], 3))
questions.solution_code:
def count_subarrays_sum_k(nums, k):
    prefix_count = {0: 1}
    prefix_sum = 0
    answer = 0

    for num in nums:
        prefix_sum += num
        answer += prefix_count.get(prefix_sum - k, 0)
        prefix_count[prefix_sum] = prefix_count.get(prefix_sum, 0) + 1

    return answer

print(count_subarrays_sum_k([1,1,1], 2))
print(count_subarrays_sum_k([1,2,3], 3))

questions.title: Longest Non-Decreasing Contiguous Sequence
questions.prompt:
Given an array of integers, return the length of the longest contiguous non-decreasing sequence.

A sequence is non-decreasing if every next element is greater than or equal to the previous one.

This is not a general subsequence problem. The elements must remain contiguous.
